sections.title: A: Complete using the words in the box
sections.instructions: Use each word once: exam, fact, favour, heart, instance, mark, progress, skill.

items.sentence: My German teacher says I've made a lot of ...... this term!
What time are you taking the French ...... tomorrow?
I'm not in ...... of giving students lots of homework each night.
Being able to drive a car is a very useful ...... .
Have we got to learn all these irregular verbs by ...... ?
Some languages, like Russian for ...... , don't have words for 'a', 'an' and 'the'.
I got a very good ...... in my geography test.
Many people hate learning phrasal verbs, but in ...... they're not that difficult.

sections.title: B: Phrasal verbs
sections.instructions: Write a phrasal verb in the correct form to replace the words in bold.

items.sentence: Simon ...... the wrong answer and wrote the right one.
Why did you ...... that piece of paper?
If you make a mistake, just ...... it ...... .
You should ...... ...... words you don't know in a dictionary.
Carol, will you ...... ...... your poem to the class, please?
Our teacher ...... ...... that we only had five minutes left.
Have you all ...... ...... what the homework is?

sections.title: C: Word formation
sections.instructions: Complete by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: What's the name of Dave's driving ...... ? (INSTRUCT)
Dictionaries and encyclopaedias are examples of ...... books. (REFER)
I've only been learning Arabic for a few months, so I'm still a ...... . (BEGIN)
In maths, you have to learn to do addition, subtraction, multiplication and ...... . (DIVIDE)
Three of your answers were ...... , so you got 17 out of 20. (CORRECT)
Are you really going to take part in the singing competition? I admire your ...... ! (BRAVE)
